verses.text: Judah's sin is 'written with a pen of iron... upon the table of their heart,' engraved deep; they shall lose their heritage. Their guilt is ingrained. Sin cut into the heart cannot be erased by any human effort.

'Cursed be the man that trusteth in man... whose heart departeth from the LORD'; he is like a withered shrub in the desert. Misplaced trust dries the soul. To lean on man and forsake God leaves the heart barren.

'Cursed be the man that trusteth in man... whose heart departeth from the LORD'; he is like a withered shrub in the desert. Misplaced trust dries the soul. To lean on man and forsake God leaves the heart barren.

'Blessed is the man that trusteth in the LORD'; he is 'as a tree planted by the waters,' green and fruitful even in drought. Faith makes the soul flourish. Rooted in God, the believer thrives when others wither.

'Blessed is the man that trusteth in the LORD'; he is 'as a tree planted by the waters,' green and fruitful even in drought. Faith makes the soul flourish. Rooted in God, the believer thrives when others wither.
'The heart is deceitful above all things, and desperately wicked: who can know it? I the LORD search the heart.' Our hearts deceive even us. Only God truly knows and judges what lies within.
'The heart is deceitful above all things, and desperately wicked: who can know it? I the LORD search the heart.' Our hearts deceive even us. Only God truly knows and judges what lies within.

As a partridge broods on stolen eggs, so wealth wrongly gained is lost; the LORD is 'the hope of Israel,' and those who forsake Him shall be ashamed. Ill-gotten gain flees. To leave the fountain of living waters is sure shame.

'Heal me, O LORD, and I shall be healed,' Jeremiah prays, mocked by those who scorn God's word; let his persecutors be ashamed, not he. He casts himself on God. The faithful look to God for healing and vindication.

God bids them hallow the sabbath day and bear no burdens, but their fathers 'obeyed not.' The command is plain. Honouring God's day is a test of a heart that honours God.

If they keep the sabbath, the city shall stand for ever; if not, He will kindle an unquenchable fire in her gates. Blessing or ruin hangs on obedience. Reverence for God's holy day brings life; contempt brings fire.
